sire.name: Lady Aveline Dubois
description: 7th Generation Malkavian
Embraced shortly before the French Revolution
Anarch loyalist
High Humanity
Wanderer by nature
Derangement: Bipolar Disorder
More focused on signs or omens

convictions.description: You Must Take Care Of Yourself First
notes: Ultimately, no one has your best interest at heart except for you.
While everyone is ultimately in it for themselves and maybe for their loved ones.
But no one will prioritize you, but you.

touchstone.name: Kade Phillips
notes: Big Hearted Man who cares deeply for his family and friends.
Connects easily with others and commands love and respect, even in his less than manly profession.
He's the kind of Man Aishe raised her sons to be like.
There is a deep anger within him; he often spends time with people who stoke it.
With so many oceans of time allies may turn to foes and vice versa
Keeping a door open is useful, even if unsatisfying at the time

touchstone.name: Camille Bouschar
notes: Recent Divorcee
Survived an Unhappy Marriage for 30+ years
Stroke up a friendship with Aishe a few years before her divorce
Her Daughter, Marie, blames Aishe for her Parent's divorce
Camille Reminds Aishe of who she could have been had she not met her Sire
Keep your enemies and allies guessing.

touchstone.name: Katrine Cousteau
notes: Daughter of owner of an Occult Shop -- The Threefold Exchange, in Chinatown
While the owner is (somewhat) legit, Katrine takes advantage -- of her mother, of her customers, of everyone
Actually a full time swindler selling people "magical" solutions to their mundane problems
Katrine stokes Aishe's duplicitous nature

backgrounds.name: Status
description: As Malkavian Primogen and one of the Prince's inner circle, you are an established power in Camarilla society, with all the visibility, obligations, and deference that office demands.
Your standing is not merely ceremonial: you are recognized as part of the city's ruling structure, and others are expected to treat you accordingly.
rules: You sit at the table of power in the sect.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools in courtly or status-conscious situations.

backgrounds.name: Status
description: Within Clan Malkavian, your standing is equally formidable. You are not just respected, but understood to be one of the clan's true local powers, with the authority and responsibility that brings.
Other Malkavians are likely to look to you as one of the figures who defines what the clan is, and where it stands, in Toronto.
rules: You are a renowned representative of your clan's interests and one of its leading local figures.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools, or sometimes allow it to stand in for a Social Trait in especially status-driven clan scenes.

merits.name: Stunning
description: Your beauty is not merely striking, but disarming: the kind that makes people lean in before they realize how dangerous the conversation has become. Combined with your poise, seductiveness, and air of occult mystery, it turns every social exchange into terrain you naturally control.
rules: You gain two extra dice to appropriate Social dice pools when your appearance can affect the interaction.

merits.name: Bloodhound
description: You read people through omens, instinct, and the subtle truths they leak without meaning to, so sensing resonance in blood feels like an extension of your predatory intuition rather than a separate trick.
rules: You can smell the Resonance of a human's blood without tasting it.
You must be within olfactory range and succeed on a Resolve + Awareness test at Difficulty 3, with the Difficulty adjusted for distance, perfume, or intimate contact.

flaws.name: The Curse of the Moon
description: Your curse does not merely sharpen your eccentricities. It binds you to a permanent fracture in perception, one that has become central to how you navigate the world: you know, with absolute certainty, that the Tarot always tells you the truth. The cards are not guidance, metaphor, or comfort. They are revelation. Even when you force yourself to act against what they show, you do not stop believing them. You only find a way to live, briefly, beside the certainty.
rules: This Flaw replaces your default Malkavian clan bane with the following mechanics.

You chose a permanent Derangement when you purchased this Merit: 'Persistent Delusion'. This Derangement is always active, and instills in you the unshakable certainty that every card drawn from your Tarot deck reveals truths more absolute than anything your perceptions or experiences could provide.

You may force yourself to act against that delusion for one scene by making a number of Rouse Checks and spending Willpower equal to your Bane Severity, but you do not cease believing the delusion: In an act of mental gymnastics, you bifurcate your beliefs, compartmentalizing your certainty for a time without actually ever denying it.

flaws.name: Prey Exclusion
description: You will not feed on the addled, the unstable, or the mentally unsound. Whatever games you play with truth and uncertainty, this is one place where your cruelty stops.
rules: You refuse to hunt the addled. If you feed on them, you gain Stains as though you had violated a chronicle Tenet.
Seeing others feed on the addled without interfering may also cause Stains at the Storyteller's discretion.

flaws.name: Enemy
description: A college student and daughter of Camille Bouschard, Marie blames you for her parents' divorce (She's Right)
She's currently leading an investigation into Mara, Fomórach's eldest childe.
Her skill at getting to the truth, and convincing others to come along with her, is a looming threat.
rules: You have a Gifted Mortal enemy who will act against you when she can.
The Storyteller should bring her into play at least once per story.

bloodline.name: Descendant of the Pythia
bloodline.description:
There are few things mortals fear more than the future, for it is the ultimate unknown. And as long as that fear has existed, people have sought ways to pierce the veil of tomorrow, to embrace their destined paths or halt them entirely. Most who claim such insight are liars or charlatans.

One was not.

The Pythia, Oracle of Delphi, truly saw what was to come. She gazed upon futures both radiant and terrible, and unlike others, she learned not to fight them. She accepted them.

Chosen of Apollo, God of the Sun, the Pythia advised kings, generals, and nobles. Through dreams sent by her divine patron, the future was unveiled to her with merciless clarity.

Yet even foresight offers no sanctuary.

She vanished for thirteen nights.

When the Pythia returned to her temple, she was transformed. Her skin deathly pale, her teeth stained red. Apollo's light no longer answered her prayers. That very night, in an act of sacrilege that shattered the sanctity of Delphi, she Embraced her fellow priestesses. From this desecration was born a new lineage of vampires who see what must come, and who must endure the curse of foreknowledge.

merits.name: Seeing the Omens
merits.description: The legacy of the Pythia sings in your Blood, gifting you with an affinity for interpreting omens.
merits.rules: Gain one dot of Insight and the Insight (Omens) Specialty.

merits.name: The Dark Between the Stars
merits.description: Where the Pythias of the Oracle at Delphi used prophecy to aid others, you leverage your power to serve yourself.
merits.rules: At the end of each session, you may ask the Storyteller one question related to your Ambition or current Desire. The Storyteller will provide the answer in the form of a clue or omen before the start of the next session.

merits.name: Portent
merits.description: The mechanics of fate itself begin to reveal themselves to you.
merits.rules: At the end of each session, roll a Hunger die and set it aside. Until the end of the next session, you may replace any rolled Hunger die (whether yours or not) with the die you set aside. If doing so changes the result of the roll into a Messy Critical or a Bestial Failure, you also recover one point of Superficial Willpower damage.

merits.name: Follower of Motes
merits.description: The skeins of destiny weave through all things and into all people, and you have learned to pluck those strings.
merits.rules: At the end of each session, you may assign two dots among new Contacts, Allies, and/or Influence Backgrounds, representing strangers who do not know you, but whom your insights into the future have granted you leverage or the precise words to say to get what you want. You must choose the focus of these Backgrounds when you assign them. They persist until the end of the next session.

merits.name: The Deck of Wandering Stars
merits.description: Pythia's lineage evolved and adapted over the centuries, incorporating the prognostications of other cultures into their own. The Tarot has been a symbol of Pythian prescience for centuries, and there is no greater symbol of this affinity than the Deck of Wandering Stars, a powerful artifact of which you are the current keeper.
merits.rules: Appearing as a slightly worn set of Tarot cards, the Deck contains only the Major Arcana. Once per scene, you may perform a reading with the Deck, focusing on a specific use of Auspex that you must define at the time, and use in the same scene or in the next. If you do, gain a bonus equal to your Blood Surge bonus on any roll required for that power, and automatically succeed at any Rouse Checks required. Additionally, only fate itself can separate you from the Deck of Wandering Stars: if you lose it, or it is stolen, circumstances will conspire to return it to your hands within three days and three nights.

loresheet.name: The Cobweb
loresheet.description:
You're never truly alone. Not anymore. Not even if you want to be. Not even if you try to be.

The Cobweb catches so many thoughts in its sticky strands, sends them skittering further inside, or reverberating out to the far edges. You're not always sure the thoughts you're hearing are from now. Some feel like they've been stuck for years and have just shaken loose. Others taste like tomorrow.

The Cobweb, also known as the Madness Network, is a psychic network to which all Malkavians are linked. No two clan members describe it exactly the same way. For some, it's a constant low murmuring in their Blood. Others describe it as a kind of hivemind operating alongside their own cognition. Many, even most, Malkavians are only reminded of its presence a few times a year, like a sudden burst of static on a forgotten radio.

No one knows the Cobweb's ultimate purpose, though it's been used to summon a gathering of Oracles together with a subconscious imperative dubbed The Call. Some suspect Malkav himself uses the Network to view the world through his descendants' eyes and ears, or that he simply is the Network.

merits.name: A Break in the Static
merits.description: The Cobweb is just barely perceptible to you.
merits.rules: You catch sporadic snatches of conversation, often just a few distinct words or images. It's enough to piece together an order or a call for aid, though you're unable to respond.

merits.name: Step into My Parlor
merits.description: You can communicate over the Network with other Malkavians nearby.
merits.rules: These discussions are heavily abstract, limited to short phrases, simple images, and strong emotions.

merits.name: Across the Web
merits.description: Your voice is one of the strongest on the Cobweb.
merits.rules: You can hold more involved conversations with Malkavians in your city. Once per story you may initiate the Call, sending impressions of the time and gathering place to all who can hear. This does not guarantee obedience.

merits.name: Pluck the Strands
merits.description: Your connection to your Cainite ancestors and descendants is powerful.
merits.rules: Once per story, you may use the cobweb to piggyback on your sire's or one of your childer's senses. You are only an observer, and cannot control their responses or their movements.

merits.name: Malkav's Will
merits.description: The entity in the Cobweb is awake and aware.
merits.rules: Malkav, or a consciousness pretending to be him, speaks to you directly via the Network. Once per story, you may ask the Storyteller to divulge a secret about another Malkavian or reveal what orders the mind in the Cobweb wants you to follow.

timeline.details:
I was born in Brașov to a father with money but no standing. He treated everything as an asset, including me. When a minor noble named Nicolae Strandza needed his useless son married off to settle debts, my father saw an opportunity. The bargain was simple: legitimacy for him, solvency for Strandza, and Andrei for me.

My husband was twice my age and half my worth. A fool made respectable by a ring is still a fool. He drank, embarrassed himself, and disappointed his father as thoroughly after marriage as before it. What he did give me were three children, Elena, Vlad, and Gheorghiu, and they became the center of my life. I managed the household, shielded his reputation, dragged him to bed at night, and learned not to look when his eyes lingered on younger women.

When our youngest left for university, Andrei decided we should travel to New Orleans, claiming culture but seeking only new vices in a more exotic setting. With my children gone, I followed him out of duty and habit.

There, with little left to occupy me, I found the occult. Through it I met Lady Aveline Dubois. She offered me something I had never truly known, companionship, understanding, and the awakening of parts of myself long neglected. I wondered what it might be like to kiss her; I did not remain wondering for long.

The night before our return voyage, she invited me to her parlor. I remember little of what followed.

I died in New Orleans.

I awoke in the lodgings Andrei and I shared, reborn into hunger and clarity. He lay nearby, drunk on wine and opium, reeking of another woman’s perfume. Before he could fully see me, I tore out his throat. His blood was sweet, and my feeding was savage, my Beast taking vengeance for years of quiet humiliation. He did not deserve it, but that knowledge came later.

Lady Aveline was there when I returned to myself. The massacre proved convenient; abandoned dresses and mangled remains led the authorities to assume animals had killed us both. Even now, I refuse to feed on the addled, it reminds me too much of my mortal sorrow.

For many nights after, I remained at Aveline’s side. I learned her madness and loved her through it, through her manic brilliance and her crushing despair. From her I learned the truths of blood, and of our blood in particular. I learned divination, and soon mastered it. The cards see everything, even what we wish they would not.

Shamefully, those nights were the happiest of my unlife.

Then she was gone. I woke alone, with only a message left behind: “The Line in Blood shall meet twice. Then no more.” I did not understand then.

But I was alone now, and I had to learn how to survive.

I never truly belonged among my sire’s sect. The Anarchs could smell my upbringing on me, as if death had done nothing to strip away class, habit, or belief. Even in undeath, we carry our mortal selves longer than we ever carried our lives.

Hiding one night in the cellar of an abandoned farmstead, I fed on an escaped criminal and found a plain deck of Tarot cards. I read them, and they told me to return to New Orleans and seek the Emperor.

So I presented myself to Camarilla court. Even Anarchs must bow to Tradition. That was when I saw Prince Doran, young in manner if not in years, full of hope and possessed of a vision. In that moment, I knew my future lay with the Camarilla.

But the Ivory Tower demands its price. What I did to earn my place there is not something I will ever repeat. It hollowed me. My admission was written in blood, the ink mixed with the ashes of countless Kindred.

Now, I find myself wondering if it was ever worth the cost.

Prince Doran’s vision was vast, but New Orleans was a nest of violence and schemes. I had joined the Ivory Tower as little more than a neonate, a whisper amid the roar of Camarilla politics, and I had no one to guide me.

There was one advantage to my bloodline. Malkavians are feared for their madness, but valued for their insight. Those who knew my lineage sought my services, and I spent those years currying favor with local tyrants, rising carefully through ambition, insight, and deceit in equal measure.

It was then I crafted my persona, a carefully constructed madness that served me better than honesty ever could. I gave the powerful colorful names, riddles dressed as jokes, and let rivals exhaust themselves searching for meanings that may or may not have existed.

My visions served my goals, and my prince’s. I averted disasters, turned Garou aside, and unraveled the designs of the Magi through quiet manipulation. In the end, it mattered little. Doran met his final death in 1955, and his dream died with him.

In the aftermath, I found myself reflecting on the fragments of humanity I had left behind.

Marcel Guilbeau succeeded Doran as prince, though he ruled only in name, a puppet for his brother. Whatever New Orleans might have become died with Doran, and my own ambition dimmed for a time. I continued to play my role at court, but without conviction. Doran dreamed of a Carthage reborn, Kine and Kindred existing openly together. I see now how foolish that was. We are predators, and they are prey.

And yet, we were all Kine once.

In those years, I opened a small shop, harmless, respectable, where bored, upper-middle-class women came for palm readings and questions about love. They were vapid, lost, and sad, and they reminded me painfully of my mortal self. Unlike the usual charlatans, I gave them truths they could actually use. Some were grateful. Some were not.

I cannot say exactly what I learned during those long nights of quiet charity, but they filled a hollow I had not realized was there.

These nights mark the second time I met my sire. I found her lingering outside my little shop, and when we spoke for the first time in over a century, it felt, briefly, as though we had never parted. But I had changed. I had learned to guard my heart, and she saw it immediately.

We argued. Time had driven a wedge between us deeper than the night she abandoned me. I had forgiven her for that, but she would never forgive me for joining the Ivory Tower, for becoming the prince’s oracle. She said our Gift was never meant to serve them, that I had betrayed her.

She was older, wiser, and far freer than I had ever been, living openly, surviving on insight, charm, and truth. She still carried the same spark that once drew me in. I loved and hated her for it. She did not change. I did.

And the cruelest truth was that she was right. I am the Ivory Tower. I serve myself by serving the status quo.

When she rushed me, I thought my unlife was ending. I was never a fighter. Instead, she embraced me, not with blood, but with arms, and my heart thundered in my chest. Afterward, a postcard arrived, sent to the wrong address, bearing the image of the CN Tower. One reading was enough to understand it.

Toronto was where true dominion waited. Where I might build something lasting. I relocated quietly, my history in New Orleans earning me a place. I kept my head down.

Until now. Now, it is do or die.

sire.name: Jervais bani Tremere
notes: A key player in the behind-the-scenes affairs of Clan Tremere during its early days within Europe before the Camarilla's formation.
His loyalty to Goratrix made him a Sabbat spy in later years, and he embraced Caesar for the sole purpose of having another agent in the New World.
Seemingly met Final Death at the hands of Caesar, who loathed him for forcing him to be a double agent.

childer.name: Severin Koslov
notes: An Eastern-European immigrant who was formerly a worker with the Brotherhood of Carpenters union before his Embrace. Has the sort of wisdom one would expect from his age and humble background.

Was selected for the Embrace by Caesar due to his stubbornness. His insistence on finding out strange numbers not adding up on finances (which may have been a Masquerade breach) ended up convincing Caesar to bring him into the fold.
Currently a Ward of the Hecata who is still under Accounting, with the Bokor training him in the study of Oblivion.

convictions.description: My Soldiers Must Be Able To Handle Anything
notes: Chief Keira Vaughn is the chief of Toronto's police department who, after briefly catching a Masquerade breach before it was scrubbed (one of Mara's dramatic murders of a political official), has been investigating what she believes is a conspiracy of criminals who can commit impossible feats of strength and speed operating in the city.
Caesar admires her for how many lieutenants she's been able to bring into the fold and convince them fully.
Her stern leadership reminds him of his days as a ship captain.

touchstone.name: Keira Vaughan
notes: Chief of Toronto's police department; briefly caught a Masquerade breach before it was scrubbed (one of Mara's dramatic murders of a political official).
Has been investigating what she believes is a conspiracy of criminals capable of impossible feats of strength and speed in the city.
Caesar admires how many lieutenants she's brought into the fold and convinced fully.
Her stern leadership reminds him of his days as a ship captain.

convictions.description: The Captain Always Saves Himself First

notes: Retained from Black Caesar's days following the Path of Power and the Inner Voice during his tenure as a member of the Sabbat.
Slightly more likely to result in Stains when violated, compared to other Convictions.

backgrounds.name: Status
description: As Sheriff, your place in Camarilla society is obvious and weighty: you are an officer of Praxis, feared, visible, and entrusted to enforce the Prince's will.
Even those who dislike or distrust you must acknowledge that your office gives you real standing in the city's hierarchy.
rules: You hold recognized office in the sect.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools in court, introductions, and other status-conscious interactions.

backgrounds.name: Status
description: Among the Tremere, your standing sits far below what your age and office would normally imply. Your long history still commands a baseline of regard, but your tainted reputation and uneasy place in clan politics leave many warlocks wary of your loyalty.
In practice, you are known and grudgingly tolerated within the clan, but not embraced as one of its trusted figures.
rules: You are in good standing, but you are not especially respected and may carry a poor reputation.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools in Tremere-specific dealings, though its low rating reflects clear limits on that influence.

backgrounds.name: Herd
description: As Sheriff, you have turned the machinery of Camarilla punishment into a feeding arrangement of your own: thin-bloods and other court prisoners disappear into hidden confinement, where no one important is expected to ask questions.
rules: You have a small group of vessels from whom you can feed without concern. They can perform basic services, but they are neither as loyal nor as tightly controlled as Retainers.

You can slake up to two Hunger from the Herd each week without a roll, or more at the risk of endangering your Herd through overfeeding. Half of this Hunger is flavored with the Resonance you chose when you purchased this Background.

backgrounds.name: Mawla
description: Caesar and the Bokor both had a hand in shaping what Kindred society would look like in the Caribbean when they both meddled in the Haitian Revolution. Neither man asked too many questions about the other's "boss".

They were also both founding members of House High Saturday, with the Samedi's involvement being crucial for their understanding of Necromancy. He may not be a member today, but is still on good terms with Caesar.
rules: The Bleak Bokor is a reliable advisor, intermediary, or source of information with meaningful influence.
He can offer guidance, warnings, introductions, or speak on your behalf when appropriate.
He expects reciprocity, and the Storyteller decides the exact help he can provide in a given situation.

backgrounds.name: Contact
description: You keep Christopher Giovanni Jr. close as a frightened but useful go-between linking you to Bacchus and the Giovanni interests around you. He is young, ambitious, already half-pulled into the family's dark machinery, and terrified of disappointing monsters he hopes one day to join.
rules: You know a well-placed mortal who can provide useful information and arrange useful things for you.
This Contact is appropriate for resources or favors of moderate value within his area of access.
He mainly provides information, and may expect favors or payment in return.

backgrounds.name: Retainer
description: Jean Ducheski-Francois III is the polished public face of a century of work: a cultivated Ducheski revenant descended from Caesar's long-running effort to breed his own occult servitors. Mild-mannered, charming, and older than he looks, Jean serves less like a mere ghoul and more like a carefully refined family asset.
rules: This retainer is a loyal and capable servant who can handle competent, meaningful assistance.
This Ducheski revenant is a ghoul that should be built as an Average Mortal with Auspex 1 and Blood Sorcery 1.
Retainers are reliable, but they are still characters rather than puppets and can create complications of their own.

backgrounds.name: Retainer
description: Jezdimir Ducheski comes out of the family's Polish stronghold with more instinct than understanding, a rough-edged revenant whose hooligan manner and petty-criminal background make him unexpectedly useful in dirtier work. He is tied to the same Ducheski legacy, but he feels less like a perfected instrument and more like a dangerous tool you keep close at hand.
rules: This retainer is loyal but comparatively limited in skill and independence.
This Ducheski revenant is a ghoul that should be built as a Weak Mortal with Auspex 1 and Blood Sorcery 1.
Retainers follow your lead, but they can still betray you, fail you, or complicate scenes if treated badly or tempted.

merits.name: Bloodhound
description: You have spent centuries treating blood as material to be judged, sorted, and used, so the ability to scent its resonance suits both your discipline and your hunger for control.
rules: You can smell the Resonance of a human's blood without tasting it.
You must be within olfactory range and succeed on a Resolve + Awareness test at Difficulty 3, with the Difficulty adjusted for distance, perfume, or intimate contact.

flaws.name: Dark Secret
description: Your public story paints you as a loyal infiltrator who played the Sabbat false, but the truth is uglier: despair, manipulation, and a century of horror drew you genuinely into the Sword of Caine before you clawed your way back out.
If that truth became widely known, it would do more than embarrass you. It would recast your entire reputation as a lie and make your place in Camarilla power far harder to defend.
rules: You are hiding a grave black deed or history that would be deeply damaging if exposed.
As long as it remains secret, it grants fewer Flaw points than open Infamy would.
If it comes out, the Storyteller may treat it as Infamy or otherwise bring serious social and political consequences down on you.

flaws.name: Groveling Worm
description: You do not scourge yourself out of pure shame, but out of a twisted fusion of devotion, appetite, and self-abasement. In your appeasements to the Lwa, penance and pleasure blur together until drawing more blood than necessary becomes part of the ritual.
rules: Once per session, you must make time to scourge or otherwise abuse yourself.
When you do, you suffer two points of Superficial Health damage.
If you fail to do so, you suffer one point of Aggravated Willpower damage at the beginning of the next session.

flaws.name: Prey Exclusion
description: However monstrous your history has been, feeding on mortals themselves has become a line you will not cross. You may take vitae from the already-damned, but drinking from the living is something you now reject.
rules: You refuse to hunt mortals. If you feed on them, you gain Stains as though you had violated a chronicle Tenet.
Seeing others feed on mortals without interfering may also cause Stains at the Storyteller's discretion.

flaws.name: Shunned
description: You are not merely disliked by the Anarchs. To them, you embody some of the worst things the Camarilla can be: a Blood Leech, a hunter of the weak, and a Sheriff who turns his attention on thin-bloods and Caitiff who already live closest to the boot. Wherever your reputation reaches Anarch ears, it curdles quickly into active hostility.
rules: Anarchs completely loathe you and will work against you when they can.
This hostility is active rather than merely social, especially among those with reason or opportunity to oppose you.

flaws.name: Folkloric Block
description: Whether it comes from your years on the open ocean or from the spirit-haunted currents of House High Saturday, a line of salt strikes something old and irrational in you. It does not destroy you, but it does make your Beast recoil.
rules: When faced with a barrier of salt, you must shrink away from it or spend a point of Willpower to push through.

flaws.name: Knowledge Hungry
description: You are never content to merely command the dead. You need to understand them, catalogue them, and chase every scrap of insight they might yield, whether as allies, tools, or warnings from the other side. For you, the Lwa are not just part of your sorcery. They are an obsession that can pull your attention away from safer or more urgent concerns.
rules: You are compulsively driven to study your chosen subject.
When you encounter books, tutorials, seminars, or similar opportunities to learn about wraiths, you must pass a Difficulty 3 Willpower test to resist chasing that obsession.

flaws.name: Mark of the Betrayer
description: Whatever story the Camarilla tells about your past, your soul still carries Sabbat ruin in a form that Blood Sorcery can read. To those with the right sight, you are marked as someone who belonged to the Sword of Caine and cannot fully wash it off.
rules: Kindred with at least one dot of Blood Sorcery can perceive your mark as a sign of Sabbat affiliation.
You suffer a two-dice penalty on Social rolls against such Kindred.

flaws.name: Blood of the Betrayer
description: When your Hunger rises too high, the stain of your past stops being subtle. Your mark begins to weep blood, turning private spiritual corruption into something anyone can see.
rules: When your Hunger is 4 or higher, your Mark of the Betrayer becomes visible to everyone.
While it is visible, you suffer a two-dice penalty on all Social rolls.

bloodline.name: Bani Gwo-Samdi
bloodline.description:
Tremere doctrine on the subject of Blood Sorcery focuses on the strength of the thaumaturge's will and their mastery over Blood. Yet the relatively young magicians of House High Saturday also sought power from forces stronger than themselves: the “Lwa”, as the various houngans of the House called them, said to be spiritual intermediaries to the divine. Before the collapse of the Pyramid, most Tremere viewed the warlocks of House High Saturday as weak for seeking power via submission to these Lwa, and even more decried them for their practice of discussing the secrets of Thaumaturgy with non-Tremere.

Tonight, however, House High Saturday's most vocal detractors are dead; their clan has lost its power to compel adherence to orthodoxy; and their open trading of knowledge has given them an understanding of something most Tremere have little comprehension of: Oblivion.

Your bloodline descends from this 300-year-old tradition of Vodou willworkers, their secrets being your birthright and your undead flesh being another body in service to the great Lwa. Staying true to the naming convention that spans all the way back to the (supposed) days of House Tremere's origins as a gathering of mortal magicians, most houngans identify themselves with the honorific of “bani Gwo-samdi”, roughly meaning “of House High Saturday”.

merits.name: Friends on the Other Side
merits.description: House High Saturday members don't just rely on each other; they have the wisdom of the Lwa to seek out as well, although these spirits can be just as capricious and petty as they can be useful.
merits.rules: Once per chapter, you can meditate on a solution to a problem, providing you have enough time and the materials for enticing the proper Lwa, allowing you to roll two dice in a Skill you don't possess or adding two automatic successes in an area you already have familiarity with.

merits.name: Veves & Chants
merits.description: You learn Oblivion at the in-clan rate and do not need to drink the Blood of another Kindred to learn it.
merits.rules: When you select Oblivion Powers, you may only select powers that grant Ranks in the Spectral School of Oblivion. Upon learning your first dot of Oblivion, you also gain a one-dot Folkloric Bane or Block Flaw.

merits.name: Crossroads Bargain
merits.description: House High Saturday always had allies outside the Pyramid and at least one group of necromancy practitioners who didn't want to kill them: the Samedi of the Hecata.
merits.rules: Gain four dots to allocate between Contacts and Mawla assigned to Hecata. Calling on these Backgrounds invokes a transactional relationship, where a favor will be asked in exchange for the Hecata's aid.

merits.name: Dernière Gorgée
merits.description: When you take another Kindred's vitae, you steal a trace of their passage and leave a faint stain of your own in return. The House calls it the dernière gorgée: the "final draught" that lingers in the Blood like a bitter aftertaste, waiting for the moment the cup shatters.
merits.rules: When another vampire dies, if you have tasted their vitae since your last feeding, you immediately become aware of their Final Death. Once per story, you may burn the last of their Blood from your system as they cross over by making a Rouse Check. If you do, the vampire returns as a wraith three hours after midnight, three nights after their death, at the location where they died.

merits.name: Crise de Lwa
merits.description: While their Hecata counterparts usually take a more iron-fisted approach to dealing with wraiths, High Saturday necromancers have cultivated a loyal retinue of ghosts at their side by giving them praise and offerings. The most precious offering being a Kindred's most valuable resource: their body. You have the curse and blessing of being a “cheval”, a necromancer uniquely susceptible to possession and said to be favored by the Lwa.
merits.rules:
- Upon purchasing this Merit, replace an Oblivion power of level two or greater with "Unliving Anchor". You do not need to satisfy the Amalgam prerequisite.
- Instead of being only able to anchor one wraith to yourself, you can instead anchor a number of wraiths equal to your Oblivion dots.
- You gain a +2 bonus when using Host Spirit targeting one of them.
- Your entourage of spirits is passionate, but they are not mindless servants. Any failure on a Ceremony roll is treated as a Total Failure, resulting in one of your attendant spirits possessing you without your permission to pursue their own goals for the remainder of the scene.
notes: Jean Ducheski-Francois I: Former first-mate and revenant of Caesar's from the late 1700s. The closest wraith to Caesar as both men have known each other for 3 centuries. Very level-headed and tactically minded but also becomes overly worried about his captain easily. Can take drastic measures in an attempt at protection.

Rebecca Rossellini: A ghoul from the Giovanni gifted to Caesar that was killed in an internal spat during the early 1900s. Her mere presence makes the Hecata uncomfortable due to her hatred of her former family. Most intimidating wraith of the trio. Sees her relationship with Caesar as transactional and a path towards revenge.

Ponticulus: A sorcerer turned wraith even older than Caesar that bonded with him from a fragment of Ceoris during the 2000s. Even in death he has a steady control over magic. He supports Caesar's rebuilding of High Saturday out of the desire to control what he sees as a potential figurehead of the Clan. Gets power-hungry on behalf of Caesar.

loresheet.name: Revenant Family Ducheski
loresheet.description:
The Ducheski were once the Krevcheski, a family of scholars in Eastern Europe patronized by its nobles. The Tzimisce subjected them to years of experimentation, transforming the family into revenants, a hereditary line of ghouls born with a semblance of vampiric vitae in their veins but afflicted with a craving for the real thing. For a time, they served the clan faithfully, developing a knack for creating advanced clockwork devices for siege warfare and torture.

When war broke out between the Tremere and the Tzimisce, the Krevcheski betrayed their masters, pledged fealty to the Tremere, and took their new name. They have served the clan in secret ever since, maintaining the Warlock's libraries, sanctums, and laboratories. The family is in decline; few are permitted to continue their line and even fewer are allowed to join the clan as Kindred. At least one member of this illustrious family is at your service.

Unless stated otherwise, purchasing any level of this loresheet for the first time provides Retainer (•), representing the revenant. A Ducheski revenant is a ghoul built as a Weak Mortal that always has access to Auspex 1 and Blood Sorcery 1.

merits.name: Nourishing Blood
merits.description: While a revenant's vitae is nowhere near as potent as the Kindred, drinking it can be just as sustaining.
merits.rules: When you feed from your Ducheski revenant, they are not subject to the Human slake penalty of your Blood Potency. Feeding from them never risks a Blood Bond. The revenant is still harmed by your feeding like a human would be.

merits.name: Personal Library
merits.description: The Ducheskis hold a wealth of knowledge within their dilapidated ancestral strongholds. Your revenant has added their personal library of ancient texts to yours, increasing your understanding of the world's mysteries.
merits.rules: Choose two of these three Skills: Academics, Investigation, or Occult. Whenever you make a test with any Specialty in your chosen Skills, you receive one extra die, in addition to any applicable bonuses.

merits.name: Research Team
merits.description: You have a tight-knit family unit of three to five Ducheski revenants in your care.
merits.rules: You receive Retainer (••) to represent them. Once per story, when you task your research team with studying a new Ritual, their dedication and teamwork allow you to learn the Ritual in half of the usual learning time.

merits.name: Ritual Assistant
merits.description: Your Ducheski revenant has a special aptitude for the story of Blood Sorcery, and can help enhance your magical techniques.
merits.rules: When your revenant is present and helps you prepare a known Ritual, the Difficulty of the roll to activate it is reduced by 1. If more than one Ritual Assistant is present, the Ritual pool increases by one die for every two Ritual Assistants present after the first.

merits.name: Ducheski Invention
merits.description: You own a one-of-a-kind Ducheski creation.
merits.rules: This could be one of their infamous clockwork devices, or even a modern technological marvel designed by a revenant in your service. Give the invention a name and function, and choose a Skill. Your Storyteller will name up to three components that make its function possible. The invention is a Specialty of that Skill that provides three extra dice when the invention is used in tasks involving that Skill. If it is damaged or destroyed, any Ducheski in your service knows how to fix it, if they have all the components available.

timeline.details:
Born in the Asante Empire as the mixed race son of a soldier with no real knowledge of his mother, he is put into slavery at a young age when he is told his father has gone missing and has assumedly abandoned his duties.

At age 15 he is packed into a ship as he and thousands of others are purchased by Dutch landowners and taken to the colonies in the New World. He lives a torturous life underneath many changing masters, one of which happens to be the murderous privateer known as Edward "Blackbeard" Teach.

Almost the entirety of "Black Caesar's" mortal life was spent in slavery with half of it being as an indentured servant in the Asante Empire and the other half as a chattel slave in the Americas. He was sold from owner to owner until eventually he was set to be executed for an attempted escape which technically went as scheduled. However his death was staged as Jervais took the opportunity to recruit a nobody to serve as a loyal spy for him.

For mortals, legends of the fearsome corsair "Black Caesar" begin to spread around both the Atlantic and the Pacific as he commits atrocities on behalf of his sire for House Goratrix which are reported as if they were acts of piracy.

"I don't care whose blood you'll have me shed. I felt nothing but fear when I was alive. Now I'll give the world a name and an image to fear in return."

During the period after the Haitian Revolution, what would become House High Saturday begins to unify and formalize their interactions with "the Lwa". They skirt around the laws of the Tremere regarding teaching outsiders but the reward outweighs the risk. Caesar is assisted by a small group of clanmates, but also his Ducheski revenants and even a member of the burgeoning Samedi bloodline: the Bokor, who is instrumental to the House's understanding of Necromancy.

"Times are changing, brothers and sisters! Let us walk into this future of radiant moonlight hand in hand with gods old and new!"

Counting up all the profits from his bloody work for Jervais makes Caesar both powerful in the mortal world and within the Pyramid as he is promoted to Magister while he continues his spy work for Goratrix. As he looks at the state of his people in the Americas, he begins to grow disillusioned with his role in vampiric society. Busywork takes him from the Americas to the Prime Chantry in Vienna to report his earnings and discoveries to the Council of Seven.

"Of course, my lord. Magister Caesar bani Gwo-samdi lives to serve. I would like to make a report regarding a most intriguing Malkavian I met..."

On orders to assist Carna, the new Regent of Milwaukee, Caesar is sent as a mercenary from the Council to fight against the Lupine horde threatening to siege the city. He forms a bond with the new Prince who fought on the front lines: Terrence Merik. Saving an Apprentice by the name of Mortius from final death at the hands of a werewolf, Caesar is given a Life Boon.

Having a full breakdown, Black Caesar makes a complete turn to the Sabbat when he pursued his sire Jervais to Toledo and openly murders him. No longer abiding by the Masquerade or his spy act, he is spurred further into the Sword of Caine's philosophy as he believes the Lwa have left him without their blessings which leaves him searching for meaning within House Goratrix not just as an infiltrator but as one of their war-time leaders in Canada.

"Lilith awakened Caine... and yet we are expected to remain asleep, our eyes shut to the injustice of this profane, kine-ruled world. No more. No. More."

After surviving the Tremere Antitribu's near extinction due to the sacrifice of one of the Lwa bound to him, Caesar's loyalty in the Sabbat wanes as he begins to reattune himself to his prior faith. Making a dangerous journey from the borders of Canada to the States he cashed in his life boon with Mortius of the Milwaukee Chantry to concoct a story claiming his time in the Sabbat was a double agent plot that was masterfully crafted by the Council. When the Prime Chantry falls years later nobody can disprove this fabricated tale. With a hunger to rebuild the now basically defunct House High Saturday he makes his way to Toronto. His masterful infiltration of a Sabbat stronghold, and limited ties to the University Chantry following Scheer's fall made him the top pick for Ulrich's sheriff, a title he assumed in 2020 shortly after Ulrich became Prince. After a few years of loyal service, the Prince granted him the right of Embrace: Severin Koslov, who has since been placed as a ward of the Bleak Bokor to learn the arts of necromancy from the true masters.

"I know what a city on the brink looks like. Trust me. Toronto needs a firm hand."

clanBanes.name: The Curse of the Broken Mirror

childer.name: "Mara" Oksana Ziva
notes: Leaned into her Wendigo ancestry, joining the Sabbat and embracing the Path of Orion

Prefers to target a well-secured government official, sneaking in and violently murdering them in a spectacularly gory manner, then waiting for their security detail to arrive so she can murder them too
Rumors in Toronto of a terrorist cell, warped vigilante, or simple psychopath since Mara wants people to know she's coming: the more prepared they are, the better the hunt

childer.name: "Palis" Yusuf bin Yafa
notes: Online handle: sko1VikingsFan69
Social Nosferatu; an influencer online; VTuber streamer; hides his face via motion graphics
Rants about politics while gaming Fortnite (ironically) and League of Legends (semi-professionally): the more toxic the environment, the better the rants
Has a bond with I.Q. for being looked down upon for their use of technology despite being necessary

touchstone.name: Vincenzo "Enzo" Caruso
touchstone.description: Retired Hitman w/ Blissfully Ignorant Family
notes: Was once a Made Man for the Toronto Mafia against the wishes of his family, eventually becoming a hitman by virtue of his dedication to the Familia
Has now retired on his blood money and started a blissfully ignorant family, spending his time on charity and feeding stray dogs
Keeps a hidden phone on his person at all times, though he's almost forgotten about it by now. The Don still has his number
Fomórach has been following him since his childhood in the form of an arctic wolf (which Enzo believes to be several generations of huskies)

convictions.description: The British Must Be Destroyed (Anachronistic Conviction)

touchstone.name: Percival Cavendish
touchstone.description: Descendant of Lord who Stripped Fomórach's family of land in Ireland
notes: Descendant of Lord Cavendish who, in 1845, leapt on Fomórach's former family in Ireland the moment the potato blight made their estates vulnerable to financial takeover
In an attempt to curry favor, I.Q. approached Fomórach with the "gift" of having uncovered Percival's identity, which earned I.Q. some favor from the Nosferatu Primogen
Since then, Fomórach has been actively and with extreme pettiness ruining Percival's life
If Percival ever confronts Fomórach, only then will he learn why his life has been ruined... and then he will die

backgrounds.name: Status
description: As Nosferatu Primogen and one of the architects of the new regime, you are a major Camarilla power whose voice helps shape the direction of the city.
Your standing marks you as one of the undead who actually governs, not merely one who attends court.
rules: You sit at the table of power in the sect.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools in courtly or status-conscious situations.

backgrounds.name: Status
description: Within Clan Nosferatu, your standing rises even higher: as Drake's childe and chosen successor, you are understood to be the clan's true local center of authority.
Whatever others outside the warrens think, your clan sees you as the figure who best embodies Nosferatu power in Toronto.
rules: You are the most respected member of your clan in the city and are broadly seen as its true leader.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools, or sometimes allow it to stand in for a Social Trait in especially status-driven clan scenes.

backgrounds.name: Contact
description: You have cultivated Siobhan Kelleher, better known as UwU_byte_me, into a genuinely valuable mortal asset: a professional hacker with real access, real skill, and enough admiration for I.Q.'s online persona to stay personally invested. Through her, your reach extends into cameras, tripwires, spytech, and the nervous system of the modern city.
rules: You know a well-placed mortal who can provide valuable information and obtain expensive or difficult things for you.
This Contact is appropriate for high-value favors within her technical field of expertise.
She mainly provides information, and may expect favors, payment, or protection in return.

backgrounds.name: Herd
description: Beneath the city, you have terrorized a small circle of homeless mortals into becoming a feeding population, keeping them compliant with threats, bribery, and the miserable half-mercy of money, drugs, or vitae.
rules: You have a small group of vessels from whom you can feed without concern. They can perform basic services, but they are neither as loyal nor as tightly controlled as Retainers.

You can slake up to two Hunger from the Herd each week without a roll, or more at the risk of endangering your Herd through overfeeding. Half of this Hunger is flavored with the Resonance you chose when you purchased this Background.

backgrounds.name: Chasse
description: You hold a small hunting claim within P.A.T.H., carved out beneath the Full Claim over the District held by Bee's Hive. At present, however, your rights there are politically snarled. The dispute with the Line has suspended the practical benefits of the claim, leaving you with contested standing instead of secure advantage.
rules: You hold a subinfeudated domain within a larger claim, and it would normally function as a Minor Claim.
Because the domain is under dispute, the usual bonuses from that claim are currently suspended.
Those benefits return only once the dispute is resolved to the Storyteller's satisfaction.

merits.name: Side Hustler
description: You survive by knowing how to squeeze value from the margins: tunnels, drifters, debts, favors, and whatever else the city forgets to count. Your power does not rest on comfort or legitimacy, but on a lifetime of making do, calling in markers, and finding what you need through informal channels.
rules: Once per session, you can get access to an item, information, or a temporary edge as though you had two dots in an appropriate Resources, Contacts, or Influence Background.
What you obtain usually comes with strings attached and often needs to be returned intact.

flaws.name: Starving Decay
description: Your body always seems only one bad night away from becoming the corpse it truly is. Hunger does not merely weaken you; it strips away the last illusion of vitality and reveals something ancient, famished, and half-feral beneath. For you, starvation is physically debilitating, and makes your monstrosity even harder to conceal as your reserves run low.
rules: Whenever your Hunger is 3 or higher, your body shrivels and decays.
While in that state, you take a two-dice penalty to physical tests and to all Social interactions with mortals, and you may risk the Masquerade.

flaws.name: Living in the Past
description: You have survived for centuries, but some parts of you never left the old wound behind. Your conviction that the British must be destroyed is not quaint nostalgia, but a living hatred carried forward into nights that have long since moved on from the world that made it.
rules: You lose one die from Social test pools involving archaic beliefs or outdated morality.
This penalty does not apply with vampires of your age or older who might admire your steadfastness.

flaws.name: Enemy
description: Chief of the Toronto Police Department's Special Investigations Unit, Kiera Vaughan is currently leading an investigation into the high-profile serial killer who is in fact Mara, your eldest childe.
Her investigation is bringing her closer to the homeless community you have coerced into silence, as well as to the Kindred community as a whole.
Her skill at getting to the truth, and convincing others to come along with her, is a looming threat to your control.
rules: You have a Deadly Mortal enemy who can take useful action against you and may not be easy to evade.
The Storyteller should bring her into play at least once per story.

flaws.name: Curse of the Broken Mirror
description: Your curse is not merely that you are unsettling to mortals. You are a walking breach of the Masquerade, an atavistic nightmare whose face cannot safely belong in the human world at all. Concealment itself becomes cursed. Your monstrous features resist disguise, resist softening, and resist every attempt to pass as anything but a predator dragged half-intact out of some older, harsher age.
rules: This Flaw replaces your default Nosferatu clan bane with the following mechanics.
You must take the Grotesque Flaw, you cannot purchase Looks Merits, and your undisguised appearance risks breaching the Masquerade if seen by mortal eyes.
Any attempt to conceal or hide your appearance suffers a penalty equal to your Bane Severity. This includes supernatural disguises such as Mask of a Thousand Faces.

flaws.name: Grotesque
description: You look less like a deformed man than like an ancestral memory of a man twisted into something predatory: bald scalp stretched taut over a heavy skull, pointed ears, lamp-like eyes, skin drawn into ridges and creases, and tusk-like fangs jutting from a mouth built wrong for speech. The effect is impossibly prehistoric, as though some half-buried terror in human memory has suddenly put on a suit.
rules: You suffer a three-dice penalty to all Social dice pools when you can be seen.

flaws.name: Dark Secret
description: The local Nosferatu know you as Drake's capable childe and successor, but buried beneath that identity is a far more dangerous truth: you descend from the Wendigo, a Red List horror everyone assumes left no living bloodline behind.
If that lineage were exposed, your standing would become entangled with an ancient monster's legacy, and suspicion would fall on you not for what you have done, but for what your Blood implies.
rules: You are hiding a grave black deed or history that would be deeply damaging if exposed.
As long as it remains secret, it grants fewer Flaw points than open Infamy would.
If it comes out, the Storyteller may treat it as Infamy or otherwise bring serious social and political consequences down on you.

flaws.name: Disease Vector
description: Your feeding does not merely exploit the weak. It carries corruption forward through them, making your hunger part of the city's physical decay as surely as its spiritual rot. For a sewer-haunting Nosferatu who preys on the desperate, sickness becomes one more thing your victims pass along after you are gone.
rules: Whenever you feed from a sick mortal, you automatically contract that sickness.
You then pass it on to the next vessel from whom you feed.

bloodline.name: Descendant of the Wendigo
bloodline.description:
In the colonial nights of the far north, across moonlit plains where those few brave souls built their outposts, the legend of the Wendigo haunted their campfires: a creature of ice and hunger, an avatar of deprivation given claws and fangs, an embodiment of famine and cold. Such was the name given to one of the first Nosferatu to journey to the New World: a feral, monstrous thing, perhaps even a wight, it nevertheless Embraced at least one other to ensure its ravenous Blood survived the ages.

Winter and hunger were its kin. Cold could not touch the Wendigo, and death by freezing did not close a meal. The Blood remembered the taste of bodies found stiff in the snow, the last thin pulse of starvation in the veins. It learned to sustain life without relieving deprivation. It learned to skinwalk, slipping into the hides of winter predators, moving as wolf or owl.

You descend from this creature of myth. Hunger and winter are your playthings, as well: You endure them both, and you make everyone else endure them, too.

merits.name: Curse of the Wendigo
merits.description: The cold and the hungry will do anything, agree to anything, to live another day.
merits.rules:
Your Blood gains the ability to sustain mortals in place of food and heat. However, this does not satiate their hunger or comfort them with warmth, it merely keeps them alive. Such mortals continue to suffer the effects of starvation or hypothermia, up to but not including the point of incapacitation.

Gain a +2 bonus to Intimidation or Persuasion rolls when exploiting the effects of this deprivation in another.

merits.name: Veins of Winter
merits.description: The Wendigo is a creature of winter as much as hunger, and your Blood carries this affinity.
merits.rules:
You are immune to the effects of cold temperatures.

Additionally, you may feed from the frozen corpses of mortals that perished from starvation or hypothermia as if they were still alive (but you cannot reduce your Hunger to 0 by feeding on that which is already dead). Such blood is always intensely ischemic and never carries a dyscrasia.

merits.name: Skinwalker
merits.description: You share the Wendigo's ability to skinwalk.
merits.rules: Gain Protean as an in-clan Discipline. You must choose a winter predator when given the choice of an animal form.

merits.name: Father of Monsters
merits.description: Whether or not the Wendigo truly was a wight, you have inherited Blood tainted with a corrupting power, which spreads to your ghouls.
merits.rules: Any ghouls that you have purchased as Retainers gain an additional Discipline dot from among those you have learned, and increase their tier by one level: RETAINER ●: Average, RETAINER ●●: Gifted, RETAINER ●●●: Deadly. If you have a famulus, your famulus' tier increases to Gifted. This power warps your ghouls physically, afflicting each with the Repulsive Flaw as they grow both gaunt and monstrous.

merits.name: Feast or Famine
merits.description: Hunger both drives and sustains you, as it did the Wendigo of legend.
merits.rules:
You gain the ability to sense the hunger and cravings of other Kindred. You know when any Kindred you can see is at Hunger 4 or above or under the effect of a Compulsion.

While fully sated at Hunger 0, your monstrous appearance relaxes into something more human: While still hideous, your appearance alone will not risk breaching the Masquerade if seen by mortals. Additionally, if you would take a Stain for any reason, you may instead increase your Hunger by one. Finally, your ability to sense hunger in others extends to knowing when another Kindred is fully sated at Hunger 0.

While starving at Hunger 5, treat your Blood Potency as one higher for all purposes. Whenever you would make a Rouse Check for any reason, make an additional Rouse Check and choose which roll to keep. Finally, you retain control during hunger frenzy and do not need to spend Willpower to act. Instead, you gain a Compulsion to feed: All actions that are not dedicated to slaking your thirst suffer a -2 penalty for the duration of the frenzy. All other mechanics of hunger frenzy still apply.

loresheet.name: The Whisper-Weaver
loresheet.description:
Kindred speak of Toronto as a city that's lost its leaders, those respected elders who heeded the call of the Beckoning, abandoning their havens and their schemes to fight a war in the east on behalf of sleeping gods. Drake's names come up as examples: the Vault, the Oubliette, and especially the Whisper-Weaver; the mysterious bogeyman in black silks who haunted the Court long before anyone ever heard of Fort York. He's gone, they say, just like Steele, like Bellanger, like so many others who once gave Toronto its sense of strength and stability.

But you? You know better. Drake may have left the city above, but he only went as far as the city below. For Toronto isn't one city, but two: the Toronto everyone sees above ground, and the Toronto beneath, a world of memories frozen in two hundred years of strata.

Down here, there are storm tunnels older than any zoning law and gaps in foundations that were built before Canada was a country. Hidden corridors, closed-off basements, abandoned subway stations and derelict utility pathways weave through this undercity. Down here, Toronto's oldest sovereign still sits his throne, unknown and unrecognized by every Kindred in the city above, save a very precious few.

You have earned Drake's trust, enough that he calls upon you, places you in important roles, and challenges you. Where other elders trade in Blood, secrets are his currency. He's careful and deliberate with every piece of information he shares. If you're searching for answers, he might reveal them, but not without a cost, and always in a way that draws you deeper into his complex web. And if there ever comes a night when Drake asks you which move he should make in the city's shadowy game, you'll know it means the story of his “leaving” has finally become more useful as a weapon than as a disguise.

merits.name: The Shadow Speaks
merits.description: You are fortunate enough to be among the few people Drake trusts.
merits.rules: In addition to being aware that the Nosferatu elder remains in Toronto, once per story, you may meet with Drake in a secluded location of his choice. There, he'll reveal what he knows about one secret you're after.

merits.name: The Labyrinth
merits.description: Drake's knowledge of Toronto's hidden paths is unparalleled.
merits.rules: Once per story, Drake will reveal a secret hard-to-detect vantage point from which you can spy on someone. Beyond that, you're on your own: make a loud noise or otherwise reveal your presence and your perfect camouflage loses its use.

merits.name: Forgotten Friends
merits.description: Drake always favoured outcasts, easily overlooked and easily dismissed.
merits.rules: They're the people that go unnoticed, like janitors, sanitation workers, or food workers. Once per story, Drake can hook you up with someone who can get you inside, or get you the info you need from the inside, for a price. The individual counts as Retainers (••) for the session, but reverts to Contacts (•) if you spend Experience to make the Background permanent.

merits.name: Whisper-Weaving
merits.description: You have gained your own talents at gathering secrets.
merits.rules: When you purchase this Merit, gain free Skill Specialties in Investigation (Espionage) and Insight (Secrets). Once per story, you can gain three pieces of secret information through your personal surveillance or close bonds with your network of spies.

merits.name: Phantom's Proxy
merits.description: Drake trusts you more than anyone else.
merits.rules: When you purchase this Merit, gain Mawla: Drake (•••). Additionally, once per story, Drake will ask your advice on what action he should take to influence Toronto's politics, and take it to make permanent change in the city.

timeline.details:
- Had lots of "spare" siblings, but more survived than expected, leaving their small patch of land unable to feed 6–8 growing young men and women.
- "Third brother had a flair for whittling; father sold three of our five cows to get him an apprenticeship as a carpenter"; he gave away heirloom jewelry to provide dowries for two sisters.
- By the time it got to Fomórach, there wasn't much left to support him; he'd have to go on his own.
- Decided to become an indentured servant so he could get a plot of land in the New World, "the golden land".
- Arrived in the New World circa 1785, to a terribly mismanaged colony that was obvious to him even before he arrived, on the boat.
- Realized that everyone was a Puritan, which made him uncomfortable given he was a Catholic.
- Drake was attracted to him as a source of information, something he became very good at: getting into earshot of important people, and feeding Drake information.
- Drake ghouled him early, but he was able to keep functioning while starving even as others suffered. Drake saw his ability to gather information while under intense stress; his "resting dumb face" combined with covert intelligence made it easy for others to forget he was there and listening; and he was one of the few who were ghouled with the taint of permanent starvation who didn't abandon his mental faculties.
- After taking him to another colony further north and seeing him repeat that talent for ingratiating himself and gleaning intel despite suffering, Drake concluded he was something special.
- After Drake escaped a werewolf encounter, he began to see the value of another pair of eyes, and the fragility of his lineage as the only childe of the Wendigo. He needed to ensure his blood survived the ages.

Following the collapse of his new home, Fomórach traveled the colonies with Drake learning the ins and outs of vampirism. Hunted by puritans in the summer and haunted by hunger in winter (and werewolves all year 'round), he learned what it truly meant to be a Descendant of the Wendigo.

Newly released by his sire, Fomórach traveled by boat back to the land of his ancestors. He hoped to find his family, to lend them the fruits of his struggles: life eternal and freedom from hunger were within his ability to grant, even if the cost would be high. Instead, he found his ancestral home raped and pillaged by the British. Enraged beyond measure, Fomórach left for London and proceeded to cut a bloody swath through the neighborhoods of the perpetrators of the Famine. It was only by the mercy of the London Nosferatu Primogen that he survived, and even then he was delivered back to Toronto staked in a coffin.

After his episode in London, it took Drake time to trust Fomórach with responsibility again. Long decades were spent making him understand the risks he took and the favors he burned with his reckless actions. Drake did not take kindly to being forced to reveal Fomórach's bloodline, and needed time to not totally extinct. Fomórach did not want that; he knew then that at the time of course, intrigue. Fomórach delved deeper into the mystery of his bloodline and took steps to ensure it would not be so threatened again. Traveling to the Ukraine, he took advantage of the Holodomor to seek out a candidate with the fortitude enough not to totally accept the nature of the blood. The vampire is a beast of hunger after all, and the Wendigo is even more so. Mara was well-suited to the blood, too well suited, and Fomórach watched history repeat itself in his childe as she rejected the order and safety of the Camarilla to pursue her own personal crusade which continues to this day. His other childe, sired during the founding of Israel and the resulting Palestinian diaspora (known as the Nakba), remained loyal though his choices in other areas remains questionable.

The Camarilla runs on tradition, but favors are a close second. It was a favor, long ago, that bolstered the growth of Toronto as a bastion of kindred society in the 1850s and now 100 years later it has been called in. Transmitted through the blood like the curse of vampirism itself, it passed from sire to childe. Oliver Gagnon called for aid on behalf of his childe Lucien, and the childe of Drake answered. Picking up Lucien from the Mojave desert turned out to be the easy part. Driving back up to Toronto together...

Well.

Lets just say it was an exciting five years and leave it at that.

(The details surrounding Fomórach and Lucien's time on the road are intentionally left vague for now; they will be explored in more detail during play in a Memoriam sequence.)

Like many elders facing The Beckoning, Drake was forced to face his impermanence. Not mortality, because he had no intention of dying, but to be forced to leave one's unlife's work is hardly a pleasant thing. With this in mind, Drake began to train Fomórach into a suitable successor. No more was he merely a trusted liegbreaker and solver of problems, now he must be made ready to handle the rigors of Camarilla politics. Fomórach was involved in the process of rulership far earlier than most would expect, participating in the primogen council's meeting from behind his sire's veil for years before said sire's "beckoning" in 2022, when Fomórach took his place as Nosferatu Primogen and *de facto* leader of Clan Nosferatu in Toronto.

Drake, however, was never Beckoned, and remains hidden in the dark places beneath the city, his continued presence known to very few.

touchstone.name: Miriam Ravenscroft
touchstone.description: Curator, Royal Ontario Museum; Adjunct Professor, University of Toronto
notes: Excitable and animated in the topics of her passions, to the point of lacking tact and social graces.
Specifically the curator for Manuscripts and Rare Texts, technically subordinate to the ROM's head Co-Chief Curator, Arts and Culture, Chen Shen.
Notoriously clumsy in any circumstance outside an archive.
Secretly publishes raunchy historical fiction under a nomme de plume, and is simultaneously proud and ashamed of the quality of her work.
There are rumors floating she has recently acquired an authentic fragment of important kindred lore/mystical significance for study.

convictions.description: You Must Take Care of Your Friends First

backgrounds.name: Status
description: As Prince, your standing in Camarilla society is absolute: you are the formal source of legitimacy, office, judgment, and domain in Toronto.
Whatever private doubts others may harbor, the structure of the sect itself places you unmistakably at its apex.
rules: You rule the domain as Prince.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools in courtly or status-conscious situations.

backgrounds.name: Status
description: Within Clan Toreador, your standing is respectable but modest compared to your throne. You are an important rose because you hold Praxis, but that does not automatically make you the unquestioned heart of clan society.
The gap suggests a prince whose sect authority has outpaced his deeper roots and prestige within his own clan.
rules: Your clan considers you an average ancilla — not highly-respected, and a far cry from being among the clan's foremost figures.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools in Toreador-specific or status-conscious interactions.

backgrounds.name: Mawla
description: Damien Abanda is more than your childe: he is a century-old bond forged in flight, adversity, failure, and survival, from Revolutionary Mexico to the long shadow of your torpor.

Though he now stands with Red Flag, Damien remains a polished bridge to Anarch politics and courtly maneuvering alike, able to offer guidance, quiet warnings, and a sympathetic voice in rooms where your authority alone cannot reach.
rules: Damien is a reliable advisor, intermediary, or source of information with meaningful influence.
He can offer guidance, warnings, introductions, or speak on your behalf when appropriate.
He expects reciprocity, and the Storyteller decides the exact help he can provide in a given situation.

backgrounds.name: Mawla
description: Evanescent Blue is one of the Ravnos survivors you pulled out of the chaos near Kingston during the Week of Nightmares, tying your legend not just to witnessing clan-apocalypse, but to preserving what little of the Ravnos remained.

Now established among the Anarchs in Color Blitz, Blue can still carry news, warnings, and hard-earned perspective from the shattered Ravnos diaspora, even if open association with such remnants is politically awkward.
rules: Evanescent is a reliable advisor, intermediary, or source of information with meaningful influence.
She can offer guidance, warnings, introductions, or speak on your behalf when appropriate.
She expects reciprocity, and the Storyteller decides the exact help she can provide in a given situation.

backgrounds.name: Mawla
description: Merrick Singh is another Ravnos survivor you brought away from the Week of Nightmares, but unlike Blue, you placed him under Oliver Gagnon's care in Toronto, judging Oliver's freer temperament better suited to shelter him after that catastrophe.

Merrick is now with Oliver's band, Scarlett & the Boys, where he survives by passing as Toreador: a glittering, ambient presence in Camarilla nightlife whose oddity and charm make him useful in rooms where open Ravnos identity would be far less welcome.
rules: Merrick is a useful personal contact who can provide occasional help, introductions, or information.
His support is narrower and less politically weighty than a higher-rated Mawla's.
He expects reciprocity, and the Storyteller decides the exact help he can provide in a given situation.

merits.name: Luck of the Devil
merits.description: From your rise out of poverty into wealth, to your rescue from torpor in the Mojave Desert, your life in undoubtedly charmed... though you have begun to notice karma always demands balance.
rules: Once per session, you can redirect a misfortune directed at you to someone else close to you.
The Storyteller is the final arbiter on what counts as a proper 'misfortune'.

merits.name: Beautiful
description: Oliver Gagnon raised you as a Toreador ideal, polishing a poor boy from Lucerne into a creature of elegance, poise, and cultivated allure. Even after torpor, reinvention, and the burdens of Praxis, that beauty remains one of the first truths anyone notices about you.
rules: You gain one extra die to appropriate Social dice pools when your appearance can affect the interaction.

merits.name: Linguistics
description: You were shaped in salons, courts, and unstable domains across Europe and North America, and your bloodline's legacy only deepens that gift: you are the sort of Toreador who is expected to sound as though he belongs wherever he stands.
rules: You can fluently speak, read, and write one additional language beyond your default languages.

merits.name: Ingénue
description: You carry yourself with such polish, grace, and apparent sincerity that people often want to believe in your innocence before they have any reason to. For a prince shaped in salons and old courts, that blameless surface is not naïveté, but one of the most refined weapons in your arsenal.
rules: You add two dice to any rolls related to avoiding suspicion or deflecting blame, at the Storyteller's discretion.

flaws.name: The Curse of Rapture
description: You do not merely appreciate beauty: you can be arrested by it. As a Toreador prince shaped by salons, performance, and the cultivated power of appearances, your fixation on storytelling means a novel and impressive narrative can seize your full attention with dangerous ease.
rules: This Flaw replaces your default Toreador clan bane with the following mechanics.
When you encounter a novel, impressive example of your fixation, you must succeed on a Resolve + Composure test at Difficulty 2 + Bane Severity or become fixated on it for the scene.

While fixated, you must spend Willpower to pull yourself away from appreciating the target of your fixation, and all rolls not related to discussing, exploring, or sharing it suffer a penalty equal to your Bane Severity.

bloodline.name: Descendant of the Count of St. Germain
bloodline.description:
The Count of Saint Germain was the Enlightenment's most enduring enigma: a man who appeared in the courts of Louis XV and Peter the Great with the same youthful face and effortless grace. He was the ultimate polyglot, capable of shifting between a dozen languages with such flawless inflection that even natives claimed him as their own. His true power, however, lay in his casual intimacy with the past. To hear him speak was to be transported; he described the marriage at Cana or the councils of Nero not as a scholar, but as an eyewitness, weaving himself into the tapestry of history with a charm that made the impossible seem mundane.

Those who carry his blood are the Scheherazades of Elysium, inheriting his uncanny ability to belong everywhere and to weave tales that transcend trivialities like “truth” or “falsehood”. A descendant of the Count never sounds like a foreigner, no matter how far they travel, and their voices carry the weight of an era long since buried. They are masters of weaving tales that resonate with the soul, using their rhetorical talents to persuade and spellbind. In a world of neon and noise, they remain the captivating voice at the campfire, awing their peers with forgotten secrets and the effortless poise of a soul that has seen the rise and fall of a thousand kings and queens.

merits.name: Cosmopolitan Communicator
merits.rules: When you purchase this Merit, gain a free Language. The Linguistics Merit provides two languages per dot, instead of one. When you speak to someone in a language that is their native tongue but not the regional default, gain a +2 bonus on Etiquette rolls.

merits.name: Courtly Ties
merits.description: The Count made easy friends wherever he travelled, and you have inherited his natural gregariousness.
merits.rules: Gain up to four dots, limited by your Sect Status, to spread among Mawla Backgrounds. You cannot purchase a Mawla Background greater than two with this Merit.

merits.name: Storyteller's Gift
merits.description: When you enter Memoriam, you may bring up to five other willing people with you by telling them the story of the Memoriam, weaving them into the narrative.
merits.rules: They must be present to hear your story and, for each person you wish to include, either you or they must Rouse the Blood. During the Memoriam, these guests may participate in the story and contribute to challenges as if they were actually there. This does not actually rewrite history, however: It is a result of your flair for storytelling, nothing more. The results of the Memoriam should be reframed after the fact to explain whatever happened in terms of the reality of that historical event (e.g., tasks performed by targets of this ability were actually achieved by you, by others who were really there, or by happenstance).

merits.name: Painting the Mind's Eye
merits.description: Your tales transcend the written word and become something else entirely, something alive in the minds of others.
merits.rules: You may purchase Chimerstry as a Presence 2 power, then Fata Morgana as a Presence 3 power, without amalgam requirements, and you may purchase these even if you have already purchased those levels of Presence.

merits.name: Rousing Rhetoric
merits.description: The Count's power to charm with the spoken word was legendary.
merits.rules: When your dice pool involves Performance, Persuasion or Leadership, you can set any single die to a 10 after rolling.

loresheet.name: The Week of Nightmares
loresheet.description:
Kindred debate when exactly the Week of Nightmares fell, with some claiming it landed just prior to the turn of the millennium, while others conflate it with the supposed commencement of Gehenna. In truth, the “Week” of Nightmares sprawled across years, sweeping up several thousand vampires in its wave. During the Week, an impossibly ancient vampire arose and purged its own clan: the Ravnos. Thin-blooded vampires emerged with omens and portents as the Red Star, Anthelios, blazed in the night sky.
