You witnessed the mania of the Week of Nightmares and survived it, manipulated the chaos to your own advantage, or you studied the actions of those who did. Now you watch for signs of dooms to come.

merits.name: Oral History
merits.description: You have heard and told the tale of the Week of Nightmares countless times, and you know exactly what to embellish and which parts to conceal. Your telling of the tale is popular in Elysia, where you are sometimes invited to speak as historian and entertainer. Important Kindred from all over come to hear your retelling.
merits.rules: Add three dice to Performance dice pools to tests to tell the story.

merits.name: Ravnos Remains
merits.description: Prior to the Week of Nightmares, the Ravnos were a clan; now, they have little more than a bloodline. You know some Ravnos remnants and perhaps share their grudge against the Gangrel who left them to annihilation.
merits.rules: You have three dots of Mawla representing this group of contacts. They carry news and warnings to you, and can perhaps be convinced to cast mighty illusions once per chronicle, but association with them is frowned upon, if discovered.

merits.name: I Was There
merits.description: You didn't just hear about it, you were on the front line. Your reputation as a survivor and on-the-spot veteran of the Week of Nightmares grants you Status among Kindred historians, occultists, Ravnos, and Gehenna cultists alike.
merits.rules: Once per story, you can use this Status to earn a minor boon from one of those groups or from a prominent courtier in the domain with a sudden interest.

merits.name: The Red Star
merits.description: Anthelios (NASA designation 28978 IXION), called Wormwood, harbinger of Gehenna, still glows in the sky, to your eyes at least. You believe it looks back at you, changing you somehow.
merits.rules: Once per story, you can either reduce your Hunger to a rating of two or gain a die to the pools of one Discipline for a night by staring at the star for 10 minutes.

merits.name: Blood of Zapathasura
merits.description: The Ravnos Antediluvian met Final Death in July 1999 at the hands of parties unknown, armed with advanced weaponry and the power of the sun. Before he died, he drained vitae from all his clan, driving them to frenzy and cannibalism in his death throes. Thick, sentient ropes of his vitae stained the ground in Bangladesh where he fell. The noonday sun did not destroy it all.
merits.rules: You own a small vial containing the Blood of the Ravnos Antediluvian. What you do with this vitae is up to you, and what it does to anyone who imbibes it is up to the Storyteller.

timeline.details: Grew up poor, picked out of a crowd by pure chance (very open to Memoriam interpretation)

The Gilded Cage
Under the meticulous tutelage of Oliver Gagnon, Lucien is taught the grandeur of his lineage and home, and forged into an ideal pupil. He moved through the salons of the Heartland, a living model of his sire's impeccable tastes, internalizing the rigid protocols of the Camarilla until the weight of the "Great Game" (the geopolitical mishmash that led to World War I) dawned. In 1915, at the close of this era, Lucien Embraced Damien Abanda as his childe before their westward flight to Mexico.

Three decades of consecutive adversity began when Lucien reached Revolutionary Mexico with no support network, no Camarilla safety net, and his newly Embraced childe Damien at his side. As they moved north through unstable domains, Lucien embraced Anarch nights during the Revolt, finding both freedom and disorder, while his mentorship of Damien was increasingly undercut by the same excesses Lucien himself could not resist. By the end of this era, Lucien was believed destroyed and was left torpid beneath the Mojave sands. The exact cause of that fall remains deliberately ambiguous.

Buried in the Mojave and unable to find blood enough to rise, Lucien remained in prolonged torpor while history moved on without him. In his absence, he came to believe a Gangrel Anarch pack connected to Damien had betrayed him, and that Damien's failures of judgment helped make both the torpor and the long abandonment possible. Damien never found him; the guilt of that failure, and the realization that Lucien had been left rather than avenged, became one of the catalysts that hardened Damien and pushed him toward Red Flag. Lucien was eventually unearthed by Fomórach and carried to Toronto.

After his recovery, Lucien shed the entitled "golden child" posture of his early unlife and recast himself as a man of ideals, conviction, and discipline. Having seen Anarch disorganization firsthand, and recognizing the Camarilla as the only organized resistance to the Sabbat in that period, he chose Montreal as a proving ground to demonstrate his loyalty to the Ivory Tower and prove his Anarch era was not his final identity. He was also, true to form, drawn to where the story was most dangerous and consequential. Montreal scarred him: nightmares, terror, and direct confrontation with the Sabbat left lasting trauma. But he emerged tempered and galvanized, no longer polished courtly potential but a leader who could plausibly claim a prince's burden.

Lucien left Montreal for Toronto as the Week of Nightmares began (June 28 to July 4, 1999). Near Kingston, violence involving Gangrel and Ravnos drew him into the chaos tied to the destruction of the Ravnos Antediluvian; he survived and came away with Evanescent Blue and Merrick in tow (full incident details remain for Memoriam play). On returning to Toronto, Lucien placed Merrick under Oliver's care, judging Oliver's freer temperament better suited to shelter him. This era marks Lucien's transition from survivor-storyteller to domain architect, with personal legend and political legacy becoming inseparable.

sire.name: Laleh bint Saif (The Piece-Taker)
notes: Ultimately became the Piece-Taker of Toronto legend.

backgrounds.name: Status
description: As Seneschal, you stand among the Camarilla's ruling elite, trusted to keep the machinery of Praxis functioning and to act with the Prince's authority when needed.
Your standing places you firmly inside the city's governing structure rather than merely adjacent to it.
rules: You sit at the table of power in the sect.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools in courtly or status-conscious situations.

backgrounds.name: Status
description: Among the Banu Haqim, you are highly respected and an important representative of clan interests, but not its uncontested pinnacle. Older and more storied figures still cast long shadows over what supremacy in the clan looks like in Toronto.
Your standing is strong enough to matter decisively, but it still reads as influential rather than supreme.
rules: You are a highly respected ancilla and a significant representative of your clan's interests.
The Storyteller may add this Status to Social dice pools in Banu Haqim-specific or status-conscious interactions.

backgrounds.name: Fame
description: You are known and quietly admired within parts of Toronto's Muslim community, not as a mass celebrity, but as a familiar and respected figure whose name carries warmth, dignity, and curiosity. That visibility fits the life you have built for yourself: one rooted in faith, belonging, and the careful cultivation of trust rather than spectacle.
rules: A select subculture knows who you are and admires you.
The Storyteller may allow you to use this Fame in Social dice pools instead of another Trait in appropriate mortal-facing situations.
It also reduces the Difficulty of some Social tests against your fans and can help with hunting, depending on your predation style.

backgrounds.name: Contact
rules: You know a mortal who can do or get something cheap or common for you.
This Contact is appropriate for resources or favors of minimal value within his area of access.
He mainly provides information, and may expect favors or payment in return.

backgrounds.name: Influence
description: You have real pull in Toronto's financial world, enough that bankers, brokers, and other money-people hesitate before crossing you and are often willing to do favors. For a Vizier and Seneschal committed to building cities rather than breaking them, that influence is one of the clearest expressions of your power.
rules: You are entrenched in the financial sector, and mortal power-brokers there hesitate to oppose you.
The Storyteller may require or allow you to use this Influence in place of a Trait for some Social tests involving minor bureaucrats, functionaries, or others inside that sphere.
Your Influence mainly applies within that group and within your city, unless the Storyteller rules otherwise.

merits.name: Object of Power
description: Ur-Shulgi's Promise is the ring that came alive in the wake of revelation, prophecy, and blood-memory, the iron band that seared itself into your flesh when Michael named your burden. For you, its power is inseparable from lineage: a mark of doom, purpose, and sorcerous destiny bound directly to your body.
rules: Once per story, after rolling for a Blood Sorcery power or Ritual, you may set one Hunger die to a 10.

flaws.name: Arcane Curse
description: Your sorcery does not fail quietly. When your control slips, the curse bound up with Ur-Shulgi's Promise expresses itself through sudden sparks, embers, and heat, turning even minor mistakes into dangerous signs of supernatural instability. Every sorcerous mishap threatens to become immediate, visible destruction.
rules: Whenever you fail a Ritual or Blood Sorcery roll, sparks and hot embers shower the vicinity, risking exposure and fire.

flaws.name: Rat in a Cage
description: You are controlled, poised, and disciplined right up until confinement strips that control away. Being pinned down, bound, or physically restrained provokes a sharp, humiliating panic that makes clear how much your composure depends on freedom of movement.
rules: Whenever you are penned in or physically restrained, the Difficulty of any rolls you make under those circumstances increases by 2.

flaws.name: Dark Secret
description: You have built your identity around restraint, peacemaking, and the duty to create rather than destroy, yet that creed was born from a catastrophe you caused: in Diriyah, one impulsive assassination helped bring down an entire city.
If the full truth emerged, it would not merely stain your name. It would undermine the moral foundation on which you have rebuilt yourself ever since.
rules: You are hiding a grave black deed or history that would be deeply damaging if exposed.
As long as it remains secret, it grants fewer Flaw points than open Infamy would.
If it comes out, the Storyteller may treat it as Infamy or otherwise bring serious social and political consequences down on you.

flaws.name: Knowledge Hungry
description: You are drawn to the Iron Prince's reign not out of nostalgia alone, but because you hunger to understand how a city was made durable, orderly, and worth preserving across centuries. For a builder, seneschal, and would-be steward of Toronto's future, the Iron Praxis feels less like history than a manual you have not yet finished reading. That fascination can become consuming whenever some new source promises insight into how Steele ruled, endured, or made the city hold together.
rules: You are compulsively driven to study your chosen subject.
When you encounter books, tutorials, seminars, or similar opportunities to learn about the Iron Praxis, you must pass a Difficulty 3 Willpower test to resist chasing that obsession.

flaws.name: Burn Scars
description: The flesh around Ur-Shulgi's Promise never truly healed, leaving blackened scars running from the cursed ring up your forearm. The scars are not only disfiguring. They make other vampires recoil on instinct, as though they can smell the fire that made them.
rules: Your visible burn scars impose a penalty on Social rolls with Kindred who can perceive them, equal to the dots in this Flaw.
At one dot, the scars can usually be concealed with unobtrusive clothing choices.

flaws.name: Crisis of Identity
description: Your faith, duty, and self-conception are all bound tightly together, so losing control feels like more than failure. It feels like becoming someone unworthy of the principles you have built your unlife around.
rules: Whenever your actions result in a bestial failure, you suffer 1 point of Superficial Willpower damage in addition to the roll's other effects.

flaws.name: Vein-Tapper
description: You find feeding most natural when it is intimate, hidden, and controlled, not when it is made into a public spectacle. That preference suits your reserve, your discipline, and the quiet possessiveness with which you approach blood.
rules: You find feeding extremely personal and cannot take blood from mortals while being observed.
This usually means you feed from the unaware or go out of your way to find or create drugged or unconscious victims.

flaws.name: Resonance Sensitivity
description: You are unusually vulnerable to blood that is too calm, too settled, too emotionally still. Phlegmatic resonance catches at the part of you that craves restraint and order, then twists it into something compulsive.
rules: You are sensitive to Phlegmatic resonance.
When you feed on a vessel with that resonance at Intense or higher, the Storyteller gives you a Compulsion tied to it persists until it is shed.
If the Compulsion remains at the end of the story, it erupts in a messy, potentially violent way chosen by the Storyteller.

flaws.name: Prey Exclusion
description: Your feeding is constrained by faith as much as hunger: even after centuries, and despite all the compromises of unlife, you still refuse to prey on non-Muslims. The act of drinking blood remains entangled with the question of who may rightfully be brought inside your moral world.
rules: You refuse to hunt non-Muslims. If you feed on them, you gain Stains as though you had violated a chronicle Tenet.
Seeing others feed on non-Muslims without interfering may also cause Stains at the Storyteller's discretion.

flaws.name: Cursed Object
description: The same ring that carries prophecy also carries your ancestor's shadow. Ur-Shulgi's Promise is permanently fused to your hand, blackening the flesh beneath it and planting stray thoughts and impressions in your mind that do not feel wholly your own. The ring is both gift and intrusion: a source of power that quietly pressures you toward darker instincts.
rules: Once per session, at a time determined by the Storyteller, you must reroll one successful test and risk failure instead.

flaws.name: Curse of Obsession
description: You are obsessed with a utopian vision where everyone is on the same side.
You resist taking action against someone unless they have already rejected your best attempts at a diplomatic solution.
rules: Acting in defiance of your obsession incurs one Stain.

flaws.name: Curse of Growing Darkness
description: Like all Banu Haqim, your skin darkens with age rather than growing pale, as though undeath deepens the mark of your blood instead of draining it away. In your case the change is still subtle enough to read as unusual rather than openly supernatural, but it hints at the deeper antiquity waiting in your Blood.
rules: You gain an additional Clan Bane: Your skin grows darker with age instead of paler.
At your current age, this has no significant mechanical effect and is worth no Flaw dots.
It only becomes dot-worthy when the darkening grows visibly supernatural after centuries of unlife.

bloodline.name: Descendant of Ur-Shulgi
bloodline.description: Ur-Shulgi is spoken of as something worse than an elder: an ancient Assamite horror from the oldest wars, entombed beneath Alamut for centuries before rising as the Black Shepherd. When he woke, he judged his clan, culled the unworthy, and shattered the Tremere's centuries-old blood curse with sheer will. Those who carry his lineage inherit heat that does not fear flame, patience for plans measured in centuries, and the certainty that obedience is taken, not requested.

merits.name: The Silent King
merits.description: A sliver of Ur-Shulgi's domineering presence lives in your Blood.
merits.rules: Once per session, when you attempt to command deference from a vampire whose applicable Status is lower than yours, you may re-roll one die.

merits.name: Veins Ablaze
merits.description: You carry an inner heat that refuses to panic before flame.
merits.rules: Reduce the Difficulty of rolls to resist frenzy triggered by fire by 2 (minimum Difficulty 1).

merits.name: Architect of Eternity
merits.description: You think in centuries, laying foundations whose harvest may not be yours.
merits.rules: Once per story, when you Launch a Project during a Memoriam, the Launch Roll automatically succeeds. In the present, when spending Goal dots to reduce that Project Die, you may treat your Goal dots as doubled for that purpose.

merits.name: Herald of Destruction
merits.description: Ur-Shulgi's infamous wrath can flare through you at the perfect moment.
merits.rules: Once per session, after rolling, you may set one Hunger die to 10 if doing so would produce a Messy Critical.

merits.name: Breaker of Chains
merits.description: Your Blood remembers how to unmake bindings other Kindred accept as inevitable.
merits.rules: Gain the Unbondable Merit. Additionally, if you feed a thrall a full Rouse Check's worth of vitae, it immediately breaks their regnant's Blood Bond. You cannot create Blood Bonds of your own.

loresheet.name: Nephilim
loresheet.description: The Nephilim are a modern cult fixated on the Dream of Constantinople: a vampire “utopia” once upheld by a Trinity of philosophers (Michael, the Dracon, and Antonius) before crusade-era catastrophe and corruption tore it apart. Nephilim seek perfection as a path to rebuilding that idealized city, either as true believers preparing a new Trinity or as wary observers watching a dangerous movement gather momentum.

merits.name: To Build the Dream
merits.description: You study the people and politics required to raise a new Constantinople.
merits.rules: Gain the Persuasion (Camarilla) and Politics (Camarilla) Specialties.

merits.name: The Architect's Wisdom
merits.description: You've absorbed Antonius' lessons on governance and the structures that hold a domain together.
merits.rules: Add 2 dice to Politics pools involving domain governance. Once per story, you may decisively mediate a court dispute, defusing escalation and quashing imminent violence through action or authority.

merits.name: The Angel's Grace
merits.description: You carry the spark of Michael's ability to inspire and steady others against the Beast.
merits.rules: Add 1 die to Insight rolls to gauge another's Beast. Once per story, spend 1 Willpower to let another vampire re-roll up to 3 dice when resisting frenzy.

merits.name: The Dragon's Disciple
merits.description: You count yourself among the Dracon's disciples and know how his surviving shadow might be found.
merits.rules: The Dracon functions as a 5-dot Mawla, aiding you with mediation, compromise, and the work of forging a “new Constantinople.”

merits.name: The Trinity Reborn
merits.description: You believe prophecy names you as part of the new Trinity that will rebuild the Dream, whatever it costs.
merits.rules: Once per story, before making a Remorse test, you may remove up to 5 Stains earned in pursuit of bringing about the new Trinity.

timeline.details: In life, a former Sufi involved in the rise of Wahabism and the ensuing Ottoman-Wahabi war.

Embraced directly into the Web of Knives as a Warrior Caste spy.

Through total luck, he impulsively seized opportunity to assassinate a powerful Kindred enemy: the Ventrue Prince of Diriyah, Ibrahim Kethüda.

Unintended consequences abounded: the Prince's many agreements and negotiations keeping Diriyah's enemies at bay died with the Prince, and the city burned within a fortnight.

In this, Rashid was forced to face the scope of the suffering he could wreak with a single careless act, and began to question his role in his clan and the responsibility of power.

Rashid still keeps the ring he lifted out of the ashes that remained after he staked the Prince and left him to face the Sun, as a reminder of a mistake he will never make again.

His sire, who would become the Piece-Taker, was equally devastated; it was here that she took her famous vow of silence and began her path towards becoming the militantly isolationist autarkis of Toronto.

(Dark Secret: if this ever gets out, it would invalidate his entire professed worldview.)

After spending twenty years at Alamut trying to reconcile his clan's teachings with the disaster of Diriyah, Rashid became so disillusioned with Clan Assamite that he abandoned Alamut in search of a better path forward.

Having learned about the Dream of Constantinople in the great library at Alamut, and hearing of the Tanzimat Reforms currently underway in Constantinople, the city of Michael's Dream became his next destination in his quest to find a way to exist without being an avatar of destruction.

Rashid's interest in the Dream did not go unnoticed, and he was approached by Sofia Mavridì, a Nephilim of Clan Toreador and a descendant of Michael the Archangel himself.

First, she was an asset in Rashid's investigations into Constantinople's history, but the more time they spent together, the more convinced Rashid became that the Nephilim was the very path forward he was looking for: a path of creation, not destruction; a promise to build instead of to ruin.

Sofia was more than happy to induct him into the followers of Michael's Dream.

Devoted to pursuing Michael's Dream as a member of the Nephilim, when Rashid discovered that Sofia was a descendant of Michael himself, he recalled writings in the library at Alamut relating to travelling through ancestral blood memories.

Returning to Alamut, he suffered the presence of his despised clan to study the sorcerous teachings he needed to help Sofia unlock the memories in her blood.

Returning to Constantinople, armed with new magic, he was successful, more than successful; in a Blood Sorcery ritual, he and Sofia encountered a memory, or reflection of Michael.

The Archangel revealed the monstrous truth of Rashid's ancestor, Ur-Shulgi, but delivered a prophecy: for everything that Ur-Shulgi would destroy upon his awakening, Rashid would rebuild.

When he awoke from the ritual trance, the ring from Diriyah glowed a deep red, and Michael had given it its name: "Ur-Shulgi's Promise". With this realization, it seared his flesh and blackened his finger, binding permanently to his hand.

Having discovered the truth of his lineage, and armed with the prophecy of the Archangel, Rashid had been searching for his sire for three decades.

But Laleh bint Saif had not been seen for over a century, and the trail had long gone cold.

Finally, Rashid learned of a mysterious autarkis in Toronto, Canada, the Piece-Taker, whose description and activities were very familiar to him.

Wielding the same ritual that he used with Sofia, Rashid was able to confirm that this was indeed his sire, and so, he finally set sail across the Atlantic.

His sire took him in and sheltered him within her protected domain, where he found many of the answers he was looking for.

Rashid felt it immediately when Ur-Shulgi rose: the ring spasmed, blazed with light, and from then on he could hear the whispers of the blood god always in the back of his mind.

Knowing that this was the time that Michael's prophecy spoke of, he left the shelter of his sire's domain and quietly began planning how he could become the force for order that Michael said would balance every act of Ur-Shulgi's chaos.

After cultivating contacts and informants, he learned of the now-Banu Haqim's plan to join the Camarilla, and travelled to Denmark to assist in the forging of the Odense Pact.

Rashid returned to Toronto with a singular purpose: to finally realize Michael's Dream in the city he had claimed as his own.
